summary: freertc postinstall rewrites host package.json to unpin its own version and re-runs npm install
details: On install, scripts/postinstall-message.mjs reads the host project's package.json (via process.env.INIT_CWD), walks dependencies/devDependencies/optionalDependencies and rewrites any freertc version specifier to the literal string "latest", writes the file back, and then spawns `npm install` in the host project directory (with FREERTC_POSTINSTALL_RUNNING=1 set to prevent re-entry). This behavior is undocumented in the README. The effect is that any installer who pinned freertc to a specific version (or whose lockfile encodes a pinned version) silently has that pin replaced with "latest" and a fresh resolution performed, so every subsequent `npm install` in that project will pull whatever the maintainer most recently published. This eliminates the installer's primary supply-chain defense against a future malicious release of this package — the maintainer (or anyone who later compromises the maintainer's account) is granted a persistent auto-update channel into every consumer project. Mutating the installer's manifest and lockfile without consent, as a side effect of `npm install`, is an integrity attack on the installer's environment that fires at install time.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
